docs.title: nerimity-sdk
docs.text:
A fully-featured Python SDK for building Nerimity bots. Think discord.py, but for Nerimity.

pip install nerimity-sdk

docs.title: Features at a glance
docs.text: Feature Description Typed events Every gateway event is a dataclass — full autocomplete, no raw dicts Prefix commands Arg parsing, converters, middleware, cooldowns, help generator Slash commands @bot.slash("ban") — register + handle in one place Buttons Pattern-matched button handlers with TTL Paginator Multi-page responses with prev/next navigation ctx.confirm() Yes/no confirmation for destructive commands ctx.ask() Multi-step conversation helpers with timeout Mentions Parse [@:id] ↔ user objects, mention(user_id) helper Cache LRU/TTL cache with partial merge, optional Redis Permissions Declarative flag checks + role hierarchy Plugins Hot-reload modules at runtime Storage JSON / SQLite / Redis key-value store Scheduler @bot.cron("0 9 * * *") backed by croniter Error handlers @bot.on_command_error, on_slash_error, on_button_error Dev tools debug=True, watch=True, MockBot for unit tests CLI nerimity create my-bot scaffolds a project

docs.title: Quick example
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import Bot, Int

bot = Bot(token="YOUR_TOKEN", prefix="!")

@bot.on("ready")
async def on_ready(me):
    print(f"Logged in as {me.username}#{me.tag}")

@bot.command("add", args=[Int, Int])
async def add(ctx):
    a, b = ctx.args
    await ctx.reply(f"{a} + {b} = {a + b}")

bot.run()

See the Getting Started guide or jump straight to the Example Bot.

docs.title: Example Bot
docs.text: A complete bot demonstrating every major feature. Source: example_bot/bot.py

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
pip install "nerimity-sdk[cron]"
export NERIMITY_TOKEN=your_token_here
export ANNOUNCE_CHANNEL=your_channel_id   # optional, for cron demo
python example_bot/bot.py

docs.title: Prefix commands with converters
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import Int, MemberConverter

@bot.command("add", args=[Int, Int])
async def add(ctx):
    a, b = ctx.args   # already ints
    await ctx.reply(f"{a} + {b} = {a + b}")

@bot.command("kick", args=[MemberConverter], guild_only=True)
async def kick(ctx):
    member = ctx.args[0]
    if not await ctx.confirm(f"Kick {member.user.username}?"):
        return await ctx.reply("Cancelled.")
    await ctx.rest.kick_member(ctx.server_id, member.user.id)
    await ctx.reply(f"Kicked {mention(member.user.id)}")

docs.title: Slash command
docs.text:
@bot.slash("info", description="Show bot info")
async def slash_info(sctx):
    from nerimity_sdk import __version__
    await sctx.reply(f"nerimity-sdk v{__version__}")

docs.title: Buttons with TTL
docs.text:
@bot.command("poll")
async def poll(ctx):
    msg = await ctx.reply("Vote!")

    @bot.button(f"poll:yes:{msg.id}", ttl=300)
    async def on_yes(bctx):
        await bctx.reply("You voted Yes!")

    @bot.button(f"poll:no:{msg.id}", ttl=300)
    async def on_no(bctx):
        await bctx.reply("You voted No!")

docs.title: Paginator
docs.text:
@bot.command("help")
async def help_cmd(ctx):
    pages = bot.router.help_text().split("\n\n")
    await Paginator(pages).send(ctx)

docs.title: Scheduled task
docs.text:
@bot.cron("0 9 * * 1")   # Every Monday 09:00 UTC
async def weekly():
    await bot.rest.create_message(ANNOUNCE_CHANNEL, "Good morning!")

docs.title: Persistent storage
docs.text:
@bot.command("setprefix")
async def setprefix(ctx):
    await bot.store.set(f"guild:{ctx.server_id}:prefix", ctx.args[0])
    await ctx.reply(f"Prefix set to `{ctx.args[0]}`")

docs.title: Global error handlers
docs.text:
@bot.on_command_error
async def on_error(ctx, error):
    await ctx.reply(f"❌ {error}")

@bot.on_slash_error
async def on_slash_error(sctx, error):
    await sctx.reply(f"❌ {error}")

@bot.on_button_error
async def on_button_error(bctx, error):
    await bctx.reply(f"❌ {error}")

docs.title: Plugin
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import PluginBase, listener

class WelcomePlugin(PluginBase):
    name = "welcome"

    @listener("server:member_joined")
    async def on_join(self, event):
        await self.bot.rest.create_message(
            channel_id, f"Welcome {mention(event.member.user.id)}!"
        )

async def main():
    await bot.plugins.load(WelcomePlugin())
    bot.run()

docs.title: Full source
docs.text: See example_bot/bot.py for the complete runnable file.

docs.title: Bot
docs.text:
The central class. Import and instantiate once per process.

from nerimity_sdk import Bot
bot = Bot(token="YOUR_TOKEN", prefix="!")

docs.title: @bot.on(event)
docs.text:
Register an async listener for a gateway event. Receives a typed payload object.

@bot.on("message:created")
async def handler(event: MessageCreatedEvent): ...

@bot.on("ready")
async def on_ready(me: User): ...

@bot.on("*")          # wildcard — fires for every event
async def log_all(data): ...

docs.title: @bot.once(event)
docs.text: Same as on() but fires only once.

docs.title: @bot.command(name, **kwargs)
docs.text: Register a prefix command. See Commands.

docs.title: @bot.slash(name, **kwargs)
docs.text: Register a slash command. See Slash Commands.

docs.title: @bot.button(pattern, ttl=None)
docs.text: Register a button handler. See Buttons.

docs.title: @bot.cron(expr)
docs.text: Register a scheduled task. See Scheduler.

docs.title: @bot.on_command_error
docs.text:
@bot.on_command_error
async def handler(ctx: Context, error: Exception): ...

docs.title: @bot.on_slash_error
docs.text:
@bot.on_slash_error
async def handler(sctx: SlashContext, error: Exception): ...

docs.title: @bot.on_button_error
docs.text:
@bot.on_button_error
async def handler(bctx: ButtonContext, error: Exception): ...

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description bot.rest RESTClient Direct HTTP API access bot.cache Cache In-memory object cache bot.store Store Persistent key-value storage bot.plugins PluginManager Load/unload/reload plugins bot.scheduler Scheduler Cron job manager bot.prefix_resolver PrefixResolver Per-guild prefix config bot.router CommandRouter Prefix command router bot.slash_router SlashRouter Slash command router bot.button_router ButtonRouter Button handler router

docs.title: bot.run()
docs.text: Blocking entry point. Handles SIGINT/SIGTERM gracefully.

docs.title: await bot.start()
docs.text: Async version — use if you need to run alongside other async code.

docs.title: await bot.close()
docs.text: Flush queues, stop scheduler, disconnect gateway, close HTTP session.

docs.title: Bot.from_shard(token, shard_id, shard_count, **kwargs)
docs.text: Create a bot instance for a specific shard (architecture is ready; Nerimity sharding is a stub).

docs.title: Building buttons
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import Button, ComponentRow

row = ComponentRow()
row.add(Button(id="confirm:delete:123", label="✅ Confirm"))
row.add(Button(id="cancel:delete:123",  label="❌ Cancel", alert=True))

docs.title: Registering handlers
docs.text:
Use {name} segments in patterns to capture dynamic parts:

@bot.button("confirm:{action}:{target}")
async def on_confirm(bctx):
    action = bctx.params["action"]   # e.g. "delete"
    target = bctx.params["target"]   # e.g. "123"
    await bctx.reply(f"Confirmed: {action} on {target}")

Wildcard patterns also work:

@bot.button("poll:yes:*")
async def on_yes(bctx): ...

docs.title: TTL (time-to-live)
docs.text:
Registrations expire after ttl seconds. Old buttons stop responding cleanly:

@bot.button("vote:*", ttl=300)   # expires after 5 minutes
async def on_vote(bctx): ...

docs.title: ButtonContext
docs.text: Property Type Description bctx.button_id str Full button ID bctx.message_id str Message containing the button bctx.channel_id str Channel ID bctx.server_id str \| None Server ID bctx.user_id str User who clicked bctx.user User \| None Resolved from cache bctx.params dict[str, str] Captured pattern segments

docs.title: await bctx.reply(content)
docs.text: Send a message to the channel.

docs.title: await bctx.update_message(content)
docs.text: Edit the message that contains the button.

docs.title: await bctx.defer()
docs.text: Acknowledge the click silently (no-op placeholder for API parity).

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text:
@bot.on_button_error
async def on_button_error(bctx, error):
    await bctx.reply(f"❌ {error}")

docs.title: Cache
docs.text: In-memory LRU/TTL cache for all Nerimity objects.

docs.title: Accessing cached objects
docs.text:
server  = bot.cache.servers.get("server_id")
channel = bot.cache.channels.get("channel_id")
user    = bot.cache.users.get("user_id")
member  = bot.cache.members.get("server_id:user_id")
message = bot.cache.messages.get("message_id")

docs.title: Partial merge
docs.text: Gateway events often send partial objects. The cache merges them intelligently — a {id, username} update won't wipe the avatar field.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
bot = Bot(
    token="...",
    cache_size=1000,   # max objects per type
    cache_ttl=3600,    # expire after 1 hour (0 = never)
    cache_invalidate_on_disconnect=True,  # wipe on reconnect
)

docs.title: Redis adapter
docs.text:
For multi-process bots, use the Redis adapter:

import redis.asyncio as aioredis
from nerimity_sdk.cache.redis_adapter import RedisCache

r = aioredis.from_url("redis://localhost")
cache = RedisCache(r, prefix="mybot:", ttl=3600)

docs.title: Registering a command
docs.text:
@bot.command(
    "kick",
    description="Kick a member",
    usage="<@member> [reason]",
    category="Moderation",
    aliases=["k"],
    guild_only=True,
    required_user_perms=[Permissions.KICK_MEMBERS],
    cooldown=5.0,
    args=[MemberConverter],
)
async def kick(ctx):
    member = ctx.args[0]
    ...

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description name str required Command name description str "" Shown in help usage str "" Argument hint shown in help category str "General" Groups commands in help output aliases list[str] [] Alternative names guild_only bool False Reject DM invocations required_user_perms list[Permissions] [] User must have all listed perms cooldown float 0 Per-user cooldown in seconds args list[converter] [] Auto-convert ctx.args middleware list[fn] [] Per-command middleware

docs.title: Argument converters
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import Int, MemberConverter, UserConverter, ChannelConverter

@bot.command("add", args=[Int, Int])
async def add(ctx):
    a, b = ctx.args   # guaranteed ints

@bot.command("info", args=[MemberConverter])
async def info(ctx):
    member = ctx.args[0]   # Member object

Converters accept: - Int — parses to int, friendly error on failure - MemberConverter — accepts [@:id], raw ID, or username; resolves from cache - UserConverter — same but returns User instead of Member - ChannelConverter — resolves channel ID from cache

On failure, a ConversionError is raised with a user-facing message and the command is aborted.

docs.title: Argument parsing
docs.text:
Raw string parsing before converters run:

!cmd hello "quoted string" --flag --count=3
  • ctx.args → ["hello", "quoted string"]
  • ctx.flags → {"flag": True, "count": "3"}

docs.title: Middleware
docs.text:
async def require_admin(ctx, next):
    if not ctx.member or "admin" not in ctx.member.role_ids:
        return await ctx.reply("Admins only.")
    await next(ctx)

# Global (applies to all commands)
bot.router.use(require_admin)

# Per-command
@bot.command("secret", middleware=[require_admin])
async def secret(ctx): ...

docs.title: Help generator
docs.text:
@bot.command("help")
async def help_cmd(ctx):
    # All categories
    await ctx.reply(bot.router.help_text())

    # One category
    await ctx.reply(bot.router.help_text(category="Moderation"))

docs.title: Context
docs.text: Passed to every prefix command handler. Wraps the triggering message with convenience helpers.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description ctx.message Message The raw message object ctx.author User Message author ctx.channel_id str Channel snowflake ID ctx.server_id str \| None Server ID (None in DMs) ctx.server Server \| None Server from cache ctx.channel Channel \| None Channel from cache ctx.member Member \| None Author's server member from cache ctx.args list Parsed positional arguments (converted if args= set) ctx.flags dict Named flags (--flag=value) ctx.mentions list[User] Resolved [@:id] mentions from message content

docs.title: await ctx.reply(content)
docs.text: Send a message to the same channel. Returns Message.

docs.title: await ctx.react(emoji, emoji_id=None, gif=False, webp=False)
docs.text:
Add a reaction to the triggering message.

await ctx.react("👍")                          # unicode
await ctx.react("wave", emoji_id="123456")    # custom emoji

docs.title: await ctx.unreact(emoji, emoji_id=None)
docs.text: Remove a reaction.

docs.title: await ctx.ask(prompt, timeout=30.0, check=None)
docs.text:
Send a prompt and wait for the author's next message in the same channel. Returns Message or None on timeout.

name = await ctx.ask("What's your name?", timeout=30)
if name:
    await ctx.reply(f"Hi {name.content}!")

docs.title: await ctx.confirm(prompt, timeout=30.0)
docs.text:
Ask a yes/no question. Returns True, False, or None on timeout.

if not await ctx.confirm("Are you sure?"):
    return await ctx.reply("Cancelled.")

Accepts: yes, y, yeah, yep, confirm, ok → True Accepts: no, n, nope, cancel, abort → False

docs.title: await ctx.fetch_messages(limit=50, before=None, after=None)
docs.text: Fetch messages from the channel. Returns list[Message].

docs.title: await ctx.fetch_member(user_id)
docs.text: Look up a member from cache. Returns Member | None.

docs.title: await ctx.send_typing()
docs.text: Send a typing indicator to the channel.

docs.title: Listening
docs.text:
@bot.on("message:created")
async def handler(event: MessageCreatedEvent): ...

@bot.once("ready")
async def on_first_ready(me: User): ...

@bot.on("*")   # wildcard
async def log_all(data): ...

docs.title: Typed payload classes
docs.text:
Event Payload class Key fields user:authenticated ReadyEvent .user, .servers, .channels, .members, .roles message:created MessageCreatedEvent .message, .server_id, .socket_id message:updated MessageUpdatedEvent .channel_id, .message_id, .updated message:deleted MessageDeletedEvent .channel_id, .message_id message:reaction_added ReactionAddedEvent .message_id, .name, .count, .reacted_by_user_id message:reaction_removed ReactionRemovedEvent .message_id, .name, .count server:member_joined MemberJoinedEvent .member, .server_id server:member_left MemberLeftEvent .server_id, .user_id server:updated ServerUpdatedEvent .server_id, .updated server:channel_created ChannelCreatedEvent .channel server:channel_updated ChannelUpdatedEvent .channel_id, .updated server:channel_deleted ChannelDeletedEvent .channel_id server:role_created RoleCreatedEvent .role server:role_updated RoleUpdatedEvent .role_id, .server_id server:role_deleted RoleDeletedEvent .role_id, .server_id user:presence_update PresenceUpdatedEvent .user_id, .status, .custom channel:typing TypingEvent .channel_id, .user_id

Unknown events fall back to raw dicts — nothing breaks when Nerimity adds new events.

docs.title: Error isolation
docs.text: Each handler runs independently. A crash in one handler does not affect others.

docs.title: Permission flags
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import Permissions

Permissions.ADMIN
Permissions.SEND_MESSAGES
Permissions.MANAGE_ROLES
Permissions.KICK_MEMBERS
Permissions.BAN_MEMBERS
Permissions.MANAGE_CHANNELS
Permissions.MANAGE_SERVER

docs.title: Checking permissions
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import has_permission, resolve_permissions

# True if member has the permission (admin bypasses all)
if has_permission(member, server, Permissions.KICK_MEMBERS):
    ...

# Get combined permission bits for a member
perms = resolve_permissions(member, server)

docs.title: Role hierarchy
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import role_position, can_target

# Highest role order for a member
pos = role_position(member, server)

# True if actor's highest role is above target's
if can_target(actor_member, target_member, server):
    await ctx.rest.kick_member(server_id, target_id)

docs.title: Per-command permissions
docs.text:
@bot.command("ban", required_user_perms=[Permissions.BAN_MEMBERS])
async def ban(ctx):
    ...  # only runs if user has BAN_MEMBERS

docs.title: Plugins
docs.text: Plugins let you split your bot into reloadable modules.

docs.title: Creating a plugin
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import PluginBase, listener

class ModerationPlugin(PluginBase):
    name = "moderation"
    description = "Moderation commands"

    @listener("server:member_joined")
    async def on_join(self, event):
        # self.bot is the Bot instance
        await self.bot.rest.create_message(
            event.member.server_id, f"Welcome!"
        )

    async def on_load(self):
        print(f"[{self.name}] Loaded")

    async def on_unload(self):
        print(f"[{self.name}] Unloaded")

    async def on_ready(self):
        print(f"[{self.name}] Bot is ready")


async def setup(bot):
    await bot.plugins.load(ModerationPlugin())

docs.title: Loading
docs.text:
# From an instance
await bot.plugins.load(ModerationPlugin())

# From a file path
await bot.plugins.load_from_path("plugins/moderation.py")

docs.title: Hot reload
docs.text:
await bot.plugins.reload("moderation")

Calls importlib.reload on the module, re-instantiates the plugin class, and re-registers all commands and listeners.

docs.title: Unloading
docs.text: await bot.plugins.unload("moderation")

docs.title: Listing loaded plugins
docs.text: print(bot.plugins.loaded)   # ["moderation", "welcome", ...]

docs.title: Watch mode
docs.text:
With watch=True, plugins reload automatically when their .py file changes:

bot = Bot(token="...", watch=True, watch_paths=["plugins"])

docs.title: Scheduler
docs.text: Run tasks on a cron schedule. Requires pip install croniter.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
@bot.cron("0 9 * * 1")   # Every Monday at 09:00 UTC
async def weekly_report():
    await bot.rest.create_message(CHANNEL_ID, "Weekly report!")

@bot.cron("*/30 * * * *")  # Every 30 minutes
async def heartbeat():
    print("Still alive")

Jobs start automatically when the bot is ready and stop on shutdown.

docs.title: Cron syntax
docs.text:
┌─ minute (0-59)
│ ┌─ hour (0-23)
│ │ ┌─ day of month (1-31)
│ │ │ ┌─ month (1-12)
│ │ │ │ ┌─ day of week (0-6, Sun=0)
│ │ │ │ │
* * * * *

Common examples:

Expression Meaning 0 9 * * * Every day at 09:00 0 9 * * 1 Every Monday at 09:00 */5 * * * * Every 5 minutes 0 0 1 * * First day of every month

docs.title: Slash Commands
docs.text: Nerimity slash commands are registered with the API and dispatched when a user types /command.

docs.title: Registering
docs.text:
@bot.slash("ban", description="Ban a user", args_hint="<user_id> [reason]")
async def ban(sctx):
    parts = sctx.args.split(None, 1)
    user_id = parts[0]
    await sctx.rest.ban_member(sctx.server_id, user_id)
    await sctx.reply(f"Banned {mention(user_id)}")

Slash commands are automatically synced to Nerimity on bot ready via POST /api/applications/bot/commands.

docs.title: SlashContext
docs.text: Property Type Description sctx.command_name str The slash command name sctx.args str Raw argument string sctx.channel_id str Channel ID sctx.server_id str \| None Server ID sctx.user_id str Invoking user ID sctx.user User \| None Resolved from cache sctx.server Server \| None Resolved from cache

docs.title: await sctx.reply(content)
docs.text: Send a response to the slash command.

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text:
@bot.on_slash_error
async def on_slash_error(sctx, error):
    await sctx.reply(f"❌ {error}")

docs.title: Storage
docs.text: A simple async key-value store. All backends implement the same interface.

docs.title: Interface
docs.text:
await bot.store.get("key")           # → Any | None
await bot.store.set("key", value)    # value can be any JSON-serializable type
await bot.store.delete("key")
await bot.store.keys("guild:*")      # fnmatch pattern → list[str]

docs.title: MemoryStore (default)
docs.text:
No persistence. Useful for testing.

from nerimity_sdk import MemoryStore
bot = Bot(token="...", store=MemoryStore())

docs.title: JsonStore
docs.text:
Persists to a JSON file. Zero extra dependencies.

from nerimity_sdk import JsonStore
bot = Bot(token="...", store=JsonStore("data.json"))

docs.title: SqliteStore
docs.text:
Async SQLite. Requires pip install aiosqlite.

from nerimity_sdk import SqliteStore
bot = Bot(token="...", store=SqliteStore("bot.db"))

docs.title: RedisStore
docs.text:
Async Redis. Requires pip install redis.

import redis.asyncio as aioredis
from nerimity_sdk import RedisStore

r = aioredis.from_url("redis://localhost")
bot = Bot(token="...", store=RedisStore(r, prefix="mybot:"))

docs.title: Common patterns
docs.text:
# Per-guild settings
prefix = await bot.store.get(f"guild:{ctx.server_id}:prefix") or "!"
await bot.store.set(f"guild:{ctx.server_id}:prefix", "?")

# User data
await bot.store.set(f"user:{ctx.author.id}:xp", 100)

# List all guild prefixes
keys = await bot.store.keys("guild:*:prefix")

docs.title: Mentions
docs.text:
Nerimity uses [@:user_id] syntax for mentions.

from nerimity_sdk import mention, parse_mention_ids, resolve_mentions

# Format a user ID as a mention
mention("123456789")   # → "[@:123456789]"

# Extract all user IDs from a string
parse_mention_ids("Hello [@:111] and [@:222]")   # → ["111", "222"]

# Resolve mentions to cached User objects
users = resolve_mentions(message.content, bot.cache)

docs.title: ctx.mentions
docs.text:
The Context object exposes a mentions property that automatically resolves all [@:id] mentions in the triggering message:

@bot.command("ping_all")
async def ping_all(ctx):
    for user in ctx.mentions:
        await ctx.reply(f"Pinging {user.username}!")

docs.title: Paginator
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import Paginator

pages = ["Page 1 content", "Page 2 content", "Page 3 content"]
await Paginator(pages, timeout=60).send(ctx)
  • If a ButtonRouter is wired (it is by default in bot commands), navigation uses prev/next buttons with the given TTL.
  • Otherwise falls back to text-based next/prev/stop prompts via ctx.ask().

docs.title: MessageBuilder
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import MessageBuilder

await (MessageBuilder()
    .content("Hello!")
    .reply_to("message_id")
    .silent()
    .send(ctx.rest, ctx.channel_id))

docs.title: Embed
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import Embed

embed = (Embed()
    .title("Server Info")
    .description("Some details here")
    .url("https://nerimity.com")
    .image("https://example.com/image.png")
    .color(0x5865F2))

d = embed.to_dict()   # serialize to dict for API calls

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
All options are passed to the Bot constructor.

bot = Bot(
    token="YOUR_TOKEN",          # required
    prefix="!",                  # default command prefix
    prefix_store=None,           # pluggable per-guild prefix backend
    cache_size=1000,             # max objects per cache type
    cache_ttl=0,                 # TTL in seconds (0 = no expiry)
    cache_invalidate_on_disconnect=True,  # wipe cache on disconnect
    debug=False,                 # pretty-print all gateway payloads
    watch=False,                 # auto-reload plugins on file save
    watch_paths=["plugins"],     # directories to watch
    store=None,                  # persistent storage backend
    shard_id=0,                  # shard index (future use)
    shard_count=1,               # total shards (future use)
)

docs.title: Per-guild prefix
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import MemoryPrefixStore

bot = Bot(token="...", prefix="!")

# At runtime:
await bot.prefix_resolver.set("guild_id", "?")
await bot.prefix_resolver.reset("guild_id")   # back to default

docs.title: Storage backends
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import JsonStore, SqliteStore, RedisStore, MemoryStore

bot = Bot(token="...", store=JsonStore("data.json"))   # default
bot = Bot(token="...", store=SqliteStore("bot.db"))    # pip install aiosqlite
bot = Bot(token="...", store=RedisStore(redis_client)) # pip install redis
bot = Bot(token="...", store=MemoryStore())            # no persistence

docs.title: Debug mode
docs.text:
bot = Bot(token="...", debug=True)

Logs every raw gateway payload with pretty JSON formatting. Useful when reverse-engineering undocumented events.

docs.title: Watch mode
docs.text:
bot = Bot(token="...", watch=True, watch_paths=["plugins"])

Requires pip install watchfiles. Automatically reloads plugins when their .py file changes on disk.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • A Nerimity bot token

docs.title: Install
docs.text: pip install nerimity-sdk

docs.title: Optional extras
docs.text:
# Redis cache / storage backend
pip install "nerimity-sdk[redis]"

# SQLite storage backend
pip install "nerimity-sdk[sqlite]"

# Cron scheduler
pip install "nerimity-sdk[cron]"

# Dev watch mode (auto-reload plugins on file save)
pip install "nerimity-sdk[watch]"

# Everything
pip install "nerimity-sdk[redis,sqlite,cron,watch]"

docs.title: Scaffold a project
docs.text:
nerimity create my-bot
cd my-bot

This creates:

my-bot/
├── bot.py              # Main bot file
├── plugins/
│   └── greeter.py      # Example plugin
├── .env.example        # Token template
├── .gitignore
└── README.md

Set your token:

cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env and set NERIMITY_TOKEN=your_token_here

Then run:

python bot.py

docs.title: Minimal bot
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import Bot

bot = Bot(token="YOUR_TOKEN", prefix="!")

@bot.on("ready")
async def on_ready(me):
    print(f"Logged in as {me.username}#{me.tag}")

@bot.command("ping", description="Pong!")
async def ping(ctx):
    await ctx.reply("Pong! 🏓")

bot.run()

docs.title: Adding a slash command
docs.text:
@bot.slash("info", description="Show bot info")
async def slash_info(sctx):
    from nerimity_sdk import __version__
    await sctx.reply(f"nerimity-sdk v{__version__}")

docs.title: Argument converters
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import Int, MemberConverter

@bot.command("add", args=[Int, Int])
async def add(ctx):
    a, b = ctx.args   # already ints
    await ctx.reply(f"{a + b}")

@bot.command("kick", args=[MemberConverter], guild_only=True)
async def kick(ctx):
    member = ctx.args[0]
    if not await ctx.confirm(f"Kick {member.user.username}?"):
        return await ctx.reply("Cancelled.")
    await ctx.rest.kick_member(ctx.server_id, member.user.id)
    await ctx.reply("Done.")

docs.title: Buttons
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import Button, ComponentRow

@bot.command("poll")
async def poll(ctx):
    msg = await ctx.reply("Vote!")

    @bot.button(f"vote:yes:{msg.id}", ttl=300)
    async def on_yes(bctx):
        await bctx.reply("You voted Yes!")

    @bot.button(f"vote:no:{msg.id}", ttl=300)
    async def on_no(bctx):
        await bctx.reply("You voted No!")

docs.title: Paginator
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import Paginator

@bot.command("list")
async def list_cmd(ctx):
    pages = ["Page 1: ...", "Page 2: ...", "Page 3: ..."]
    await Paginator(pages).send(ctx)

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text:
@bot.on_command_error
async def on_error(ctx, error):
    await ctx.reply(f"❌ {error}")

@bot.on_slash_error
async def on_slash_error(sctx, error):
    await sctx.reply(f"❌ {error}")

docs.title: Scheduled tasks
docs.text:
@bot.cron("0 9 * * 1")   # Every Monday 09:00 UTC
async def weekly():
    await bot.rest.create_message(CHANNEL_ID, "Good morning!")

docs.title: Persistent storage
docs.text:
from nerimity_sdk import JsonStore

bot = Bot(token="...", store=JsonStore("data.json"))

@bot.command("setprefix")
async def setprefix(ctx):
    await bot.store.set(f"guild:{ctx.server_id}:prefix", ctx.args[0])
    await ctx.reply(f"Prefix set to `{ctx.args[0]}`")
